scenario: Lattice Bank deploys a Camunda 8.8 DMN suite that scores 4500 loan applications per day. A second decision table consumes the score by referencing the first decision id, and the modeling team must pick a safe id format so the reference keeps resolving after deployment to the cluster.
question: Which decision id format keeps the result of the scored decision addressable from a dependent decision?

options.text: An alphanumeric camelCase id such as loanScore, optionally with the underscore character separating word segments
A kebab-case id such as loan-score that matches the BPMN process id style used elsewhere in the Lattice Bank workspace
A space-separated id such as Loan Score that matches the user-facing name shown in the properties panel header
A slash-separated id such as lattice/score that namespaces the decision under the workspace folder hierarchy

a.text: Correct. The DMN docs require the decision id to be an alphanumeric string and allow the underscore symbol as the only extra character, and they recommend camelCase or snake_case for multi-word ids.

b.text: Incorrect. The DMN docs explicitly forbid the kebab-case format on the decision id because hyphens are operators, so a hyphenated decision id cannot be referenced from a dependent decision.

c.text: Incorrect. Whitespace is listed as a forbidden special character in the decision id, so a space-separated id breaks evaluation lookups from a dependent decision.

d.text: Incorrect. Slashes are special characters and the DMN docs allow only alphanumerics plus the underscore symbol, so a dependent decision could not resolve the result.

explanation: The decision id must be an alphanumeric string and may include the underscore symbol; camelCase or snake_case is recommended for multi-word names. Kebab-case and other special characters are not permitted because they prevent the result from being accessed by a dependent decision.
